rulings.Case Title: PATRINA HARRISON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Patrina Harrison Iso Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 1, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Continued to April 22, 2025, on the court's motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MAHDI BOUKHAROUBA VS. 3850 18TH STREET HOUSING ASSOCIATES, L.P., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Excessive Discovery Requests; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 1, 2025 Line 7. DEFENDANTs 3850 18TH STREET HOUSING ASSOCIATES, L.P.,and BRIDGE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT COMPANY's Motion For Protective Order Regarding Excessive Discovery Requests. Defendants 3850 18th Street Housing Associates, L.P., Bridge Property Management Company's Motion For Protective Order Regarding Excessive Discovery Requests is DENIED. Defendants' meet-and-confer declaration does not show a "reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion." (CCP 2016.040.) Defendants attach a terse meet-and-confer email, in which their counsel demanded the plaintiff withdraw all propounded discovery and called the entire set "egregiously excessive  and meant to harass, annoy, and burden the defendant." (Balbo decl. Ex. 7.) The email was conclusory and made no good faith attempt to informally resolve any specific issues raised in the motion now before the court. For instance, Defendants' counsel did not mention Plaintiff's purportedly impermissible service of over 35 special interrogatories. Nor did she explain that she considered many requests in this set to be redundant with the plaintiff's previous set. The email reads like a discovery auto-reply-a generic response that covers any and all discovery demands that the sender wants to avoid. That, for certain, is not a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion. The court does not find that Defendants acted with substantial justification in bringing this motion, and the court orders Defendants and counsel, jointly and severally, to pay Plaintiff $900 in sanctions, payable to Plaintiff's counsel by April 18, 2025. Defendants are advised that stacking discovery motions is disfavored. Stacked motions may be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GERI GARCIA VS. HEBREW HOME FOR AGED & DISABLED DBA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 1, 2025 Line 11. 1 - DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR AGED & DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME & REHABILITATION CENTER DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Hebrew Home for Aged & Disabled's Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is ordered OFF CALENDAR. A party demurring to a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] demurrer." (CCP section 430.41 (a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (CCP section 430.41(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (CCP section 430.41(3).) Defendant here emailed Plaintiff's counsel but failed to show he met-or made any effort to meet-in person, by telephone or by video conference. Due to Defendant's failure to satisfy the statutory requirements, the motion is ordered off calendar. Defendant is granted 20 days leave to respond to the First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff shall forthwith lodge a proposed order consistent with this tentative. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GERI GARCIA VS. HEBREW HOME FOR AGED & DISABLED DBA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 1, 2025 Line 11. 2 - DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR AGED & DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME & REHABILITATION CENTER MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Hebrew Home for Aged & Disabled's Motion to Strike the First Amended Complaint is ordered OFF CALENDAR. A party moving to strike a pleading or a part of a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] motion." (CCP section 435.5(a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (CCP section 435.5(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (CCP section 435.5(3).) Defendant here emailed Plaintiff's counsel but failed to show he met-or made any effort to meet-in person, by telephone or by video conference. Due to Defendant's failure to satisfy the statutory requirements, the motion is ordered off calendar. Defendant is granted 20 days leave to respond to the First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff shall forthwith lodge a proposed order consistent with this tentative. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Extension Of Time To File Answer
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 1, 2025 Line 10. DEFENDANT ALLISON WARREN's Motion For Extension Of Time To File Answer. Off calendar. This motion lacks a filed proof of service and is untimely under CCP 1005. Movant also failed to provide courtesy copies, as required by SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
